title: Streptosema verrucosa

There is no plant called Streptosema verrucosa. However, I found that Streptosolen jamesonii or Streptosolen jamesonii  'Streptosolen jamesonii' is sometimes referred to as  'Streptosema' in some sources although this is not widely accepted.

However, I did find information on Streptosolen jamesonii. 

Streptosolen jamesonii, also known as the 'Pink Trumpet Vine', is a flowering plant native to South America. 

Streptosolen jamesonii is often used in traditional medicine, and its health benefits are attributed to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. 

The bioactive constituents of Streptosolen jamesonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Streptosolen jamesonii are teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
